name: GDR Pattern Reader
description: Read numbered click patterns from GDR replays in-game.

percent-from.name: Section from (%)
percent-from.description: Start of the analyzed level section

percent-to.name: Section to (%)
percent-to.description: End of the analyzed level section

player-filter.name: Player filter
player-filter.description: Which player's jump inputs to analyze

level-length-sec.name: Level length (seconds)
level-length-sec.description: Optional full level duration used by section analysis. Leave 0 to use the replay file.

timeline-frame-offset.name: Overlay: manual frame offset
timeline-frame-offset.description: Adds this many GD frames to the frame-counter playhead. Positive moves the playhead later in the replay; negative moves it earlier.

practice-resync-delay-frames.name: Practice Resync Delay Frames
practice-resync-delay-frames.description: How many game frames to wait after Practice Mode, StartPos, or checkpoint spawns before snapping the overlay to the stable current percentage.

timeline-game-fps.name: Overlay: game FPS/TPS
timeline-game-fps.description: Your active in-game FPS/TPS. The overlay converts game ticks to GD 240-frame time using 240 / this value. Set 360 when playing at 360 FPS.

timeline-frame-scale.name: Overlay: frame scale
timeline-frame-scale.description: Fine-tune multiplier applied after game FPS conversion. Leave 1.0 first; use tiny changes only if drift remains.

timeline-resync-tolerance.name: Overlay: resync tolerance (frames)
timeline-resync-tolerance.description: Minimum frame difference before practice/start/checkpoint events move the cursor. Normal gameplay never runs percentage resync.

timeline-debug-overlay.name: Overlay: show sync debug
timeline-debug-overlay.description: Shows current level % and current replay frame on the overlay HUD.

timeline-window-sec.name: Timeline window (seconds)
timeline-window-sec.description: How many seconds of Geometry Dash time the strip shows (uses 240 TPS, same as in-game physics). Scrolls with the playhead.
